user_query: Update mothers teriyaki salmon bowl lunch to a tofu based dish

updates.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 1 > Mother > Day 2 > Lunch > Japanese Teriyaki Salmon Donburi

nutrition_note.from: Salmon is rich in omega-3 fatty acids supporting heart and brain health; broccoli provides fiber, vitamin C, and sulforaphane; rice offers sustained energy. Teriyaki glaze contains sodium and sugar — make homemade to control amounts. Pickled ginger aids digestion.
nutrition_note.to: Tofu provides high-quality plant-based protein, calcium (if calcium-set), and is lower in saturated fat than salmon. Replacing salmon removes a source of omega-3 fatty acids; consider adding ground flaxseed, a small side of edamame, or a drizzle of sesame oil to boost healthy fats if desired. Using low-sodium soy sauce and moderating the glaze sugar keeps sodium and added sugars in check. Paired with broccoli and rice, this bowl remains balanced for energy, fiber, and protein.

short_description.from: Pan-seared salmon fillet glazed with homemade teriyaki sauce, served over a bowl of steamed rice with steamed broccoli and pickled ginger.
short_description.to: Crispy pan-fried or baked tofu glazed in a savory-sweet teriyaki sauce, served over steamed rice with steamed broccoli and pickled ginger for a satisfying plant-based donburi.

meal_suggestion.from: Pan-sear salmon skin-side down until crispy, then glaze with a mix of soy sauce, mirin, and a touch of sugar. Serve over steamed rice with steamed broccoli and a small mound of pickled ginger. Garnish with sesame seeds and sliced nori.
meal_suggestion.to: Press extra-firm tofu to remove excess moisture, then cut into thick slices or cubes. Marinate briefly in a mix of low-sodium soy sauce, mirin, and grated ginger. Pan-fry or bake the tofu until golden and slightly crisp, then brush with a homemade teriyaki glaze (soy sauce, mirin, a touch of sugar or maple syrup, and mirin reduced) until caramelized. Serve the glazed tofu over steamed rice with steamed broccoli and a small mound of pickled ginger. Garnish with toasted sesame seeds and sliced nori or scallions. For a lighter option, use baked tofu and reduce the glaze sugar; for extra umami, add a splash of mushroom or kombu broth to the glaze.

updates.reasoning: Transformed the salmon donburi into a tofu-based donburi. Rewrote name, description, meal suggestion, ingredients, and nutrition note to reflect the change from salmon to tofu and updated nutritional considerations.
